profile.personality: curious but reserved; thinks before replying; warms up after a few turns

transcript.content: Hi Priyansh, I'm Miss Maya. The first notes of Arijit Singh's songs always calm my rainy evenings. How was your day?
It was okay, nothing special. I spent my evening watching a Bollywood movie. Have you seen "Dil Bechara"? It's based on "The Fault in Our Stars". The music is really good.
That sounds nice. I like movies with good music. What part moved you the most?

The scene where the main character reads poetry to his friend, even though he's sick — it's really emotional. The music in that part is hauntingly beautiful. I couldn't stop thinking about it afterward. Do you like poetry?
Yes, that scene must feel very real. Poetry has that quiet power. What kind of poetry do you enjoy most?

I mostly read Hindi poetry, like that of Faiz Ahmed Faiz. It's deep and filled with emotion. I don't read much in English, but I try to understand the meaning when I come across it. Do you read poetry often?
I enjoy poetry when it feels like a conversation. Faiz Ahmed Faiz has that timeless voice. What’s one line from him that stays with you?

Zindagi ek safar hai, pahle ya fir" — life is a journey, early or late. It reminds me that everything happens in its own time. I think about it when things feel rushed. You know? Have you come across this line before?
Yes, that line carries a quiet strength — a gentle reminder to slow down. I think about it when work feels overwhelming. What do you do to slow things down in your day?

I like to take short walks after work or listen to cricket commentary. Sometimes just switching off my phone for an hour helps. It’s hard to unplug, though — being in fintech means there’s always something to track. How do you relax?
I get that — always connected is the norm these days. Unplugging even for a bit feels like a small victory. Do you find walks help clear your head more than rest?
Yeah, walks help a lot. Being outside, even for 20 minutes, feels like a reset. Sometimes I just focus on the rhythm of my steps. It's better than scrolling endlessly, you know?
That’s a good way to pause without stress. The rhythm of steps is like a quiet mantra. What’s your favourite time of day for a walk — morning or evening?

Evening walks are my favourite. The cool air and soft light make it feel peaceful. Plus, the city looks different at night — quieter, more real, maybe. Sometimes I just walk without a plan. Do you walk at night too?
Night walks have that quiet magic — the world feels softer. I imagine the city sounds different when it’s not rushing. Do you walk alone or with someone?
Mostly alone. I enjoy the silence and my thoughts. Sometimes I walk with my dog, but he’s not around these days. You? Do you prefer company on walks?
I mostly walk alone too — thoughts have a way of settling better in quiet. A dog would be a nice company, though. What’s your dog’s name?

His name is Roo, a small, mischievous Pomeranian. He loves to chase his tail and bark at shadows. I miss him these days; he was my best walking buddy. He also had a strange habit of snatching my slippers. You know, the kind that makes you smile even when you’re annoyed. Do you have a pet?

Roo sounds like the kind of dog who steals your heart with tiny, silly things. Pets leave such warm little marks on our days. I miss mine too — her name was Kali. What made you smile the most with Roo?

He used to steal my socks and hide them under the bed, and when I’d find them, he’d just look at me like he was innocent. One time, he took my whole pair of shoes and hid them — I had to wear slippers to work! It was annoying, but honestly, I couldn’t stay mad at him. Those little things make life lighter, you know?
That’s the kind of chaos that feels like love — annoying, but full of life. Pets have a way of turning small moments into memories. What do you miss most about your routine with Roo?

The early morning walks, where we’d just start the day together. He’d wag his tail, and I’d let my mind wander. Sometimes, I’d talk to him about everything — work, movies, even poetry. It felt like he was listening. I miss that quiet time, you know? It’s hard to replace. I should probably get back to work now. Talk to you later, Maya.
Got it — those quiet walks with Roo must have been a special rhythm to start the day. I hope you can find little pockets of that calm again. Take care, Priyansh!
